title: [InstCombine] Propagate poison through fshl and fshr intrinsics
summary: The generalization strategy expands a poison-propagation optimization from a single fshl case to cover both fshl and fshr intrinsics comprehensively. Developers identified that both intrinsics propagate poison similarly—any poison operand yields a poison result—and extended the logic to handle all operand positions (first, second, shift amount) and combinations with undef. This was implemented by adding both intrinsics to `intrinsicPropagatesPoison`, enabling InstCombine to fold poison operands to poison results. The test suite was systematically expanded to validate each operand slot independently and with undef, ensuring robustness. Key insight: when an intrinsic’s semantics guarantee full poison propagation, declare it in this central utility to unlock existing folding patterns across the optimizer. This pattern is reusable: for similar intrinsics, verify poison propagation semantics and add them to this list.
